enrichment.summary: Kyle gives his broad take on religion, arguing it is inseparable from history and that blanket anti-religion stances (like those on The Young Turks) reflect ignorance of the past. He traces religion from Rome's founding through the Catholic Church's power abuses, shares extensive personal history growing up in a Tibetan Buddhist cult led by Chögyam Trungpa, and proposes a 'Religion 2.0' — neutral community centers that honor all traditions without denominational tribalism, with Rome rebuilt as a secular world cultural capital.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Historically ignorant; you cannot call something bad that has always been foundational to society
Inevitable and universal — any society rebuilding from scratch would create religion first
The problem was political power and greed, not religion itself
Cults are just bullying and authoritarianism dressed as religion; the danger is concentrating power in one leader
Greatest religion ever created but fatally flawed because it cannot evolve — needs to be replaced with a neutral, inclusive successor
Essential and constitutionally mandated; politicians invoking Christianity in government are wrong
Replace denominations with neutral 'religious community centers' open to all traditions and garb; rebuild Rome as a world cultural hub
Should be encouraged, not policed — honoring all traditions enriches society
You cannot be a truly faithful religious person without being a historian; they are the same thing
Part of why society is 'going crazy' — will settle once the trend wears off in 5–10 years
